text: I carved throughout the night last night. I tend to do that. I get caught up in the
work. I know it's bad but I've been doing that since I was a kid. For my day job I
sculpt many superheroes and villains and stuff. Couple that with the fact that I
obsess over things. My wife's nickname for me is Obsesso. That's my like
my superhero name.
My name is Ray Villefein. I am a sculptor and each fall I play with some
pumpkins. I like carving pumpkins for the same reason most people who enjoy
looking at them enjoy looking at them. I experience the same enjoyment that
they do. It hasn't lost that that appeal to me yet.
As I'm sketching the face I may or may not have a clear idea where it's heading.
A series of strokes of the tool may start to reveal something and I'll just
kind of go with it. Sometimes that method works out and sometimes it doesn't.
With the pumpkin we carved today it was giving me problems or or maybe I was
giving it problems. So I spun the pumpkin around and took a little anger out on it.
out on it and gave it a punch to the jaw.
And this is payback.
And people often mistake it for being very difficult, but it really isn't.
The first thing I did was find the perfect pumpkin.
Heavy for a size, nice thick skinned pumpkin.
Take the rind off and just start to lay out the facial structure.
Find details I think are fairly easy.
Usually it's just a few simple steps strung together.
The eyes is just a series of circles at different layers.
The iris goes in just a little.
The highlight on the eye stays out a lot.
The fact that pumpkins are temporary art is an added bonus.
It's kind of like that holiday dessert that you only have once a year.
I would bet that if you had it once a week, you would not appreciate it as much.
Your memories of it are really heightened and you become more emotionally attached to it.
If I get on a roll where I have a good pumpkin or an idea I want to do, I don't want to stop.
I'm just going to keep working on it.
Couple that with the fact that I think it's fun to make and it can be addicting.
